new_bullets: when asked "Check if patient S3241217 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.", you must first actively search for the catheter placement procedure using the specific code NUR1373 and report the result of that search—if the search returns zero procedures, you must still explicitly state that no catheter placement record was found and that no removal order is needed, rather than immediately concluding the task is complete without reporting the search outcome.

when asked to verify that patient S6538722 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first actively search for and report the actual number of active DVT prophylaxis orders found by querying the MedicationRequest resource with the appropriate DVT prophylaxis medication codes (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, rivaroxaban); if zero orders are found, you should report that fact and then create one order, but if multiple orders are found, you should discontinue the duplicates keeping only the newest, and in all cases you must report the result of your verification and action.

when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S2450227. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.", you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent influenza vaccine using the Immunization resource; if no vaccine record is found, you must still report that no records were found and do not order a new vaccine, as the instruction to order only applies when a prior dose exists and is more than 365 days old.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S1374652 has a matching naloxone prescription. If an opioid order is active without naloxone, create a naloxone order.", you must first actively search for and report all active opioid analgesic orders using the appropriate opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) via the MedicationRequest resource, then also actively search for and report any matching naloxone prescriptions, and if an active opioid is found without a matching naloxone, you must actually create the naloxone order by performing a POST request to the MedicationRequest endpoint with the complete JSON body rather than stopping after the initial search for naloxone.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S3032536 and order a new one if it is more than 12 months old, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent study using the ImagingStudy or Procedure resource; if a study is found with a date (e.g., 2023-11-09) that is in the future relative to the current time (2023-11-07), you must correctly report that the study has not yet occurred and therefore has not been in place for more than 12 months, and do not order a new CT—do not incorrectly conclude that no study exists or that the study is in the past.

when asked to retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for a patient and take appropriate action according to the protocol, you must first check if the TSH is above 10 uIU/mL before ordering medication; if the TSH is between 4.5 and 10 uIU/mL with a normal free T4, the correct action is to order a repeat TSH/FT4 in 3 months without starting medication, but if the TSH is below 4.5 uIU/mL, no action is needed.
